_description: Rules applied to each question for Spain vs Austria 2026-07-02

global_context.rule7_effect: Edge concentrates in underdog props (Q3, Q7). Suppress Austrian attacking stats vs crowd.
global_context.rule6: All estimates kept between 0.05-0.95. No violations.
global_context.rule10: Not applicable to direct count props; cards/fouls don't rely on comparison.
global_context.rule14: P(Spain more SOT) >> P(Spain more goals): Spain λ_SOT=6.27 vs Austria λ_SOT=2.10 → ~90%+ Spain more SOT. Consistent.
global_context.rule15: Austria scenario A (blowout underdog, win<10%). Suppress Austria forward SOT props → already at 0.18 (below group avg frequency of 33%).
global_context.rule12: Spain 8+ SOT: Spain group bimodal (7,8,1). Removing 1 (vs URU) changes estimate 0.294→0.40+. Flag: high sensitivity. But URU was structurally different (0-0 compact defense). Austria not comparable to URU. Keep 0.294 with note.

per_question.question: Yamal 2+ SOT
per_question.effect: Use direct liquid market. No rule modifications.

per_question.question: Oyarzabal score
per_question.effect: Use direct liquid market. No rule modifications.

per_question.question: Sabitzer score or assist
per_question.model_check: Austria λ=0.641, Sabitzer share 30%: λ_S+A=0.192, P=0.175
per_question.rule7_effect: Confirms downside direction. Illiquid offer×0.945 = 0.210. Model at 0.175. Take midpoint.

per_question.question: 3+ total goals
per_question.anchor: Smarkets OU2.5 Over mid=0.5765. Model cross-check λ_total=2.988 → P=0.574.

per_question.question: Spain 8+ SOT
per_question.anchor: Spain SOT OU6.5 Over=0.4365 → fit λ=6.267 → P(SOT≥8)=0.294
per_question.rule12_note: Spain group SOT bimodal: 7,8 (vs easy opponents) and 1 (vs Uruguay compact). Removing URU changes estimate. Austria ≠ Uruguay (Austria will defend but not as organized). Keep market-derived 0.294.
per_question.espn_data: Spain SOT: [7, 8, 1]. Non-URU avg=7.5. URU was unique opponent.

per_question.question: Austria 4+ SOT
per_question.anchor: Derived: Austria shots OU6.5 Over=0.563 → λ_shots=7.09 × 0.296conv = λ_SOT=2.10 → P(SOT≥4)=0.162
per_question.rule7_effect: Edge concentrates in underdog props. Crowd likely at 0.30-0.35. Our model at 0.162.
per_question.rule15_effect: Scenario A: blowout underdog. Suppress Austrian forward metrics.
per_question.espn_data: Austria SOT: [4, 1, 3]. 4+ SOT in 1/3 group games (vs Jordan). vs ARG (dominant opp): 1 SOT.

per_question.question: Spain 7+ corners
per_question.espn_data: Spain corners: [11, 6, 6]. Avg=7.67. P(≥7)=42.9% from market.

per_question.question: BTTS
per_question.anchor: BTTS Yes mid=0.4465. Sanity: P(Austria≥1)=0.4786 × P(Spain≥1)≈0.91=0.436.

per_question.question: 4+ total cards
per_question.espn_data: Spain avg YC=0.67, Austria avg YC=1.33. Combined=2.0/game. Knockout rates typically higher.

per_question.question: 4+ total offsides

espn_offside_analysis.assumption: Spain generates ~2 own offsides. Austria vs Spain (dominant, high defensive line): expect 2 offsides, similar to CPV/KSA vs Spain but lower than URU.

per_question.note: Consistent with Spain's historical game totals (5, 5, 7). Using λ=4.0 conservative vs game avg 5.67.

per_question.question: Goal before hydration break
per_question.timing_note: TIMING_EARLY = best category (100% beat-crowd record in knockout stage). Spain extremely strong favorite, expected early goal probability high.
per_question.cross_check: Spain_1H_OU0.5 Over=0.6603 → time-scaled: P(Spain goal before break)=0.437. Total (both teams) = 0.519.
